reasoning:
The base rate for a sitting U.S. Senator resigning or switching caucuses within a ~30-day window is historically very low (<< 1%). Fetterman is currently facing intense scrutiny following an early May 2025 New York Magazine profile alleging erratic behavior, which has prompted progressive groups in Pennsylvania to call for his resignation. He also reportedly left the Senate Democratic Caucus group chat. 

However, Fetterman has forcefully and repeatedly denied these allegations, dismissed the profile as a "hit piece," and vowed not to resign. Earlier in January 2025, he also robustly shut down rumors of a party switch or becoming an Independent (explicitly stating he would not make a "Manchin or Sinema play"). Furthermore, Democratic Senate leadership like Chuck Schumer and Dick Durbin continue to express support and work with him, effectively ruling out any forced expulsion from the caucus. Since Fetterman has already weathered the peak of the initial controversy for nearly a month without wavering, the chances of him suddenly resigning, switching, or being removed from the caucus in the remaining 33 days before July 1 are extremely low. The probability rests almost entirely on an unforeseeable severe health emergency occurring in the next month.
